core_payload.content:
# Background Agent Runtime

## 1. Definition and Essence
Background Agent Runtime is the sovereign hub that defines how N1Hub keeps long-lived agents alive outside the foreground chat loop. It now routes its doctrine through four bounded sub-hubs so daemon lifecycle, control-plane continuity, mutation permission, and execution orchestration stay explicit instead of collapsing into one flat runtime directory.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `daemon-lifecycle` owns host lifecycle, ignition, off-peak posture, model-access readiness, and cost-aware runtime bring-up.
- `control-plane` owns assignment continuity, memory state, instruction surfaces, context engineering, harness semantics, and operator-visible control.
- `mutation-gates` owns permission-to-run, workspace profiling, deep intake risk review, branch discipline, validation, and critique pressure.
- `work-orchestration` owns job envelopes, delegation, observability, stewardship maintenance, and mining/update workers.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent hub now keeps only the top-level runtime relationship to N1Hub, Personal AI Assistant, human-ai symbiosis, and its upstream project and doctrine links. Detailed runtime atoms route through the four sub-hubs so the background runtime remains legible as the sovereign envelope rather than a flat directory of runtime concerns.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Background Agent Runtime is the sovereign parent hub for four bounded runtime surfaces: daemon lifecycle, control plane, mutation gates, and work orchestration. The parent keeps only top-level runtime relationships to N1Hub, Personal AI Assistant, human-ai symbiosis, project envelopes, and doctrine anchors while detailed runtime atoms route through its four sub-hubs. This boundary matters because long-lived agent execution should remain legible as one governed runtime contract rather than collapsing daemon survival, assignment continuity, mutation permission, and job execution into one overloaded directory.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Refactored parent hub to route through 4 sub-hubs. Outbound links reduced from 44 to ≤15.
Expanded the parent summary to satisfy validator summary-length requirements and normalized the semantic hash to an eight-token canonical form without changing runtime boundaries.
